title: papaver rhoeas subsp. rhoeas
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. rhoeas, also known as the Corn Poppy, is a flowering plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, including codeine and thebaine. The Corn Poppy has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including pain, fever, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Papaver rhoeas subsp. rhoeas can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
